title: Hieracium maledictum
intro: Hieracium maledictum, also known as Devil's-bit scabious, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to Europe. Traditionally, Devil's-bit scabious has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Devil's-bit scabious is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Hieracium maledictum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe of the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a type of hawkweed, which is a genus of plants commonly found in Europe and Asia. The classification of Hieracium maledictum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, and family Asteraceae. This plant is often found growing in damp, rocky areas and is native to Scotland. Its exact classification is further specified in the binomial nomenclature system as Hieracium maledictum (Vill.) F.W. Schultz.
uses: Hieracium maledictum, also known as devil's-bit scabious, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory properties. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.
